name: Geometric Proof: (a+b)² = a² + 2ab + b²
description:
The identity (a+b)² = a² + 2ab + b² represents the area of a square with side length (a+b). Geometrically, this large square can be partitioned into four distinct sections:

  • a²: A square with side length a.
  • b²: A square with side length b.
  • ab (x2): Two identical rectangles with sides a and b.

Summing these areas gives the total area: a² + ab + ab + b², which simplifies to a² + 2ab + b². This interactive 3D surface visualizes the growth of the total area as the variables a and b change. Note how the slope increases quadratically in both directions, forming a parabolic surface known as a quadric.
